normalize: P1: Design tokens are well-defined in :root but spacing tokens use fixed px values—consider rem for accessibility scaling
P2: Type scale uses px with clamp() for H1/H2 only; body text and smaller sizes are fixed px, breaking user font-size preferences
P1: Font loading: 3 Google Fonts families loaded but some shown as 'unloaded'—need font-display: swap to prevent FOIT
P2: No design token documentation or consistent naming for semantic colors (--blue vs --cyan vs --violet lack semantic purpose names)
harden: P0: ALL 4 external GitHub links return HTTP 404—the repo 'rafayels-engineering/rafayels-engineering' does not exist
P0: Footer 'Documentation' link (#readme on 404 repo) is broken
P1: Footer 'Issues' link uses mailto:support@rafayels.engineering but is labeled 'Issues'—misleading affordance
P1: OG image at https://rafayels.engineering/og-card.png—cannot verify if this resolves
P2: Copy buttons have no visible error state if clipboard API fails (e.g., insecure context)
optimize: P3: ~38KB inline CSS in a single <style> block—could be split or minified for caching
P3: Performance is excellent (FCP 88ms, DOMContentLoaded 34ms, only 5 resources)—no critical issues
P2: Three Google Font families loaded via stylesheet—could subset or self-host to reduce external dependencies
P3: No images on the entire page—pure CSS/HTML is fast but visually sparse
adapt: P2: 7 responsive breakpoints defined (600, 700, 900, 960, 1100, 1440px) which is thorough
P1: prefers-color-scheme: light is supported but need to verify dark mode renders correctly (screenshots show light mode only)
P1: No test of keyboard-only navigation flow; focus-visible styles only confirmed on skip-link
clarify: P1: Hero subtitle text is dense—'Ship features end-to-end in Claude Code. One command takes you from idea to merged PR. Automated code review. Architecture records. Knowledge that compounds.'—too many concepts in one breath
P2: 'Three Commands to Ship' section—commands are in code blocks but lack contextual explanation of what each does
P2: '29 Specialized Agents' section lists only 8 agents with '+21 more agents' link—the truncation threshold feels arbitrary
P2: Version number 'v1.0.0' in nav is small and unlabeled—purpose unclear to new visitors

links.text: +21 more agents (View all on GitHub Marketplace)

findings: P3: All interactive elements have hover states with 0.2-0.3s transitions—good baseline
P2: Nav links use color change only on hover—subtle but adequate for nav context
P2: Primary CTA hover: background lightens (#4a88e8), translateY(-2px), box-shadow added—strong affordance
P2: Secondary CTA hover: border-color changes to blue, color brightens, translateY(-2px)—matches primary pattern
P2: Pipeline cards: border-color + background tint + icon color change per-step (violet→blue→emerald→orange→cyan)—excellent differentiation
P2: Feature cards: border-color + box-shadow + transform—consistent with card pattern
P1: Copy button hover state not visually distinct enough in screenshots—the code element hover (cyan glow) is subtle
P2: Focus-visible only confirmed on skip-link; other elements may lack visible focus rings for keyboard users

findings.description: ALL 4 external GitHub links return 404—the repository rafayels-engineering/rafayels-engineering does not exist. This breaks Marketplace, Documentation, Issues (misleadingly labeled), and Changelog footer links.
findings.recommendation: Update all external links to point to the correct GitHub repository URL. The 'Issues' link should point to actual GitHub Issues, not a mailto.

findings.description: 'View on Marketplace' CTA in the final call-to-action section links to a 404 page—this is the primary conversion path.
findings.recommendation: Fix the marketplace URL immediately. This is the most critical user journey dead-end.

findings.description: Footer link labeled 'Issues' actually opens a mailto: to support@rafayels.engineering—violates user expectations. Users expect to see GitHub Issues.
findings.recommendation: Either label it 'Email Support' or link to actual GitHub Issues page.

findings.description: Body text and component-level font sizes use fixed px values (12px, 13px, 16px). Users who set larger default font sizes for accessibility will not benefit.
findings.recommendation: Convert px-based type tokens to rem units so they respect user browser settings.

findings.description: H1 rendered text appears to concatenate 'Rafayel's' and 'Engineering' without proper spacing in the DOM (H1: 'Rafayel'sEngineering' per heading extraction)—possible span/styling issue causing missing whitespace.
findings.recommendation: Ensure a proper space or line-break element exists between the two words in the H1 markup.

findings.description: Focus-visible styles only confirmed on the skip-link. Buttons, nav links, and CTAs may lack visible focus indicators for keyboard navigation.
findings.recommendation: Add :focus-visible outlines (e.g., 2px offset blue ring) to all interactive elements.

findings.description: Page has zero images, illustrations, or visual media. The entire page is text + CSS shapes. This makes it visually monotonous despite good typography.
findings.recommendation: Add at least one hero illustration or a screenshot/diagram of the plugin workflow. Consider SVG illustrations for the pipeline steps.

findings.description: The '29 Specialized Agents' section shows only 8 agents with a '+21 more' link. The cutoff feels arbitrary and the collapsed agents lack any preview.
findings.recommendation: Show a representative sample (e.g., top 12) or add a categorized grid that gives users a sense of coverage without clicking away.

findings.description: Hero subtitle packs 4+ value propositions into one paragraph with no visual hierarchy between them.
findings.recommendation: Break into a primary tagline + supporting bullet points, or use typographic weight to create a scannable hierarchy.

findings.description: Version badge 'v1.0.0 · CLAUDE CODE PLUGIN' in the nav bar is extremely small (12px) and unlabeled—its purpose is unclear to first-time visitors.
findings.recommendation: Either make it a proper badge with context ('Plugin v1.0.0') or remove it to reduce noise.

findings.description: The 5-color pipeline accent system (violet→blue→emerald→orange→cyan) is creative but the light-mode rendering shows very subtle tints that may not register as distinct.
findings.recommendation: Increase saturation or add small color chips/dots next to pipeline step headers so the color-coding reads even at low contrast.

findings.description: Copy-to-clipboard buttons lack visible error handling if the Clipboard API is unavailable (e.g., HTTP context, older browser).
findings.recommendation: Add a fallback (select-all prompt) and a visible error toast if clipboard write fails.

findings.description: 38KB of inline CSS in a single style block—while performant for initial load, it cannot be cached separately on repeat visits.
findings.recommendation: Extract to a hashed external stylesheet for cache-friendliness on repeat visits.

findings.description: Page follows a very standard SaaS landing page pattern (hero → features → social proof → CTA). No memorable interaction or unique design moment.
findings.recommendation: Add one signature interaction—e.g., an interactive pipeline demo, a live terminal animation showing /re:feature in action, or a before/after code review comparison.

summary: The landing page demonstrates solid foundational craft—a well-structured design token system, thoughtful font pairing (Instrument Serif + Schibsted Grotesk + DM Mono), excellent performance (88ms FCP), proper semantic HTML with ARIA landmarks, and comprehensive hover states with smooth transitions. However, the site is critically undermined by ALL external links returning 404 (the GitHub repository doesn't exist), which makes the primary conversion paths completely broken. Beyond this showstopper, the page is visually monotonous with zero images or illustrations, uses fixed px units that break accessibility scaling, and follows a generic SaaS template pattern without a memorable design moment. Fix the broken links first, then add visual richness and convert units to rem.
